label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so the user wants to know what the main topic of the passage is. Let me read through the text again to make sure I get it right. The passage starts by talking about how both men and women are surrounded by media that's all about diet, exercise, fashion, and hairstyles. It mentions that men's expectations have changed from just wearing a nice suit to being more image-conscious. Then it brings up a survey from the University of the West of England where 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies, specifically issues like beer bellies and lack of muscles. The author points out that this might be due to the influence of role models on TV and in magazines. There's a mention of a muscle-enhancing shirt as a gimmick, and Joe Warner's opinion that people should just exercise instead of taking shortcuts. The passage ends with the author considering going to the gym for health and confidence, and the question to the reader about how much they worry about their physique. Looking at the options, A says not to judge by appearance. The passage does mention that beauty is skin deep and character matters, but that's a conclusion drawn rather than the main topic. B talks about obesity in England. The passage does mention obesity but it's part of the discussion about body image concerns, not the main focus. C is about role models' pressure. That's a point discussed, but again, it's part of the larger topic. D states that men are becoming more conscious of their body image. The survey and the overall discussion about men's body issues, the products like the muscle shirt, and the opinions about exercise all point to D as the main topic. The passage
